target.title: fix(memory-wiki): preserve notes after transient page reads
target.body:
﻿﻿Closes #98345

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where memory-wiki re-ingests could permanently wipe a user's hand-written `## Notes` block when the existing page re-read hit a transient failure.

## Why This Change Was Made

The memory-wiki source-page writers now retry the existing-page read once instead of converting a read failure into an empty page. That keeps the existing `preserveHumanNotesBlock()` contract active for transient read failures while still letting real repeated read failures fail closed before overwriting user-authored notes.

## User Impact

Users can keep hand-written memory-wiki notes across source re-ingests and imported-source syncs even if the first existing-page read fails transiently. OpenClaw will no longer treat that failure as "no prior notes" and write an empty notes block over user content.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: after-fix direct memory-wiki ingest preserves a hand-written `## Notes` block after a one-shot existing-page read failure, and repeated existing-page read failure fails closed without overwriting the page.
  - Command: `node --import tsx --input-type=module <redacted inline proof script>` from the PR branch worktree, using a temporary vault, real `ingestMemoryWikiSource()`, and a process-local `fs.promises.readFile` wrapper that only faults reads of `sources/roadmap.md`.
  - Observed result:

```text
[setup] vault=<temp>/vault
[setup] page=sources/roadmap.md
[setup] note-present-before=true
[transient] injected-read-failures=1
[transient] v2-content-present=true
[transient] human-note-preserved=true
[transient] page-sha=23efa6c187f1de07
[repeated] injected-read-failures=2
[repeated] failed-closed=true
[repeated] error=proof injected persistent existing-page read failure
[repeated] page-unchanged=true
[repeated] note-still-present=true
[repeated] v3-not-written=true
[repeated] before-sha=23efa6c187f1de07 after-sha=23efa6c187f1de07
```

- Claim proved: transient existing-page read failures preserve human notes for both the direct ingest writer and the imported-source shared writer.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-wiki/src/wiki-notes-read-retry.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: passed 1 Vitest shard; 1 file passed, 2 tests passed.
- Claim proved: broader touched memory-wiki note-preservation tests still pass.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest-human-notes.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-page-shared.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/vault-page-write.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/wiki-notes-read-retry.test.ts`
  - Result: passed 1 Vitest shard; 4 files passed, 14 tests passed.
- Claim proved: touched files have no whitespace errors.
  - `git diff --check`
  - Result: passed.
- Review gate:
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
  - Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`; the tool noted the branch had no unstaged diff because the fix was already committed.

Not tested / proof gaps:

- I did not reproduce the underlying OS-level `path-mismatch` race with concurrent real syncs. The real-module proof above injects the exact existing-page read failure at the changed seam, and the regression tests cover both memory-wiki writer paths around that seam.
- I did not run broad `pnpm check`, full typecheck, or full test suites per contributor-scope instructions.
target.author: qingminglong
